metric_value: If Baan continues technology-led strategy it will remain among ERP market leaders a decade hence (c. 2007)
notes: Aberdeen's key forward-looking claim: technology leadership predicts sustained market position

metric_value: Baan suffered seven consecutive quarterly losses 1998-2000; sold to Invensys for $700M in June 2000; Invensys sold to SSA Global for $135M in June 2003. Baan did not remain a market leader.
notes: Prediction dramatically incorrect. Financial misconduct and execution failures overwhelmed technology advantages. Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baan_Corporation

metric_name: SAP ERP market leadership — actual outcome
metric_value: SAP became dominant global ERP vendor post-2000; maintained and extended leadership a decade after study. SAP's R/3 evolved to mySAP then SAP ECC and S/4HANA.
notes: Aberdeen underestimated SAP's ability to overcome modeling limitations; SAP's enterprise install base and partner ecosystem proved decisive

metric_name: Oracle ERP market position — actual outcome
metric_value: Oracle acquired PeopleSoft ($10.3B 2005) and became second-largest ERP vendor; ERP cloud strategy (Fusion) emerged 2006-2011. Oracle retained major market presence despite weakest 1997 modeling capability.
notes: Oracle's acquisition strategy compensated for organic capability gap identified by Aberdeen in 1997

metric_value: PeopleSoft (excluded from study as nascent ERP player) was acquired by Oracle in hostile takeover for $10.3B in 2005 — becoming an important ERP asset despite Aberdeen's 1997 dismissal
notes: Aberdeen's exclusion of PeopleSoft proved an underestimation; PeopleSoft built strong manufacturing ERP by 2000

metric_value: OO component / internet-enabled ERP estimated as the future within 2.5-5 years
notes: Aberdeen predicted ~1999-2002 for next-generation ERP architecture

metric_name: ERP Internet/OO architecture transition — actual outcome
metric_value: SAP NetWeaver (2004) and Oracle E-Business Suite 11i (2000) delivered internet-enabled ERP; timing broadly correct but Baan did not participate meaningfully
notes: Aberdeen's 2.5-5 year estimate for internet ERP transition was broadly accurate

metric_value: Orgware can create hundreds of customer-specific production-ready BAAN IV business objects; functions and workflows from process models — shorter implementation times; reduced costs
